author: premadāsa
verses:
a, aśeṣa guṇera nidhi gaurāṅgasundara
ā, ānande vibhora sadā premera sāgara

i, indu jini’ vadanera śobhā manohara
ī, īśvara, brahmādi jā̐’re bhāve nirantara

u, uddhārilā jaga⋅jane diyā premadhana
ū, ūna pāpī⋅tāpī nāhi kailā vicāraṇa

ṛ, ṛṇa śudhibāre prabhu śrīmatī rādhāra
ṝ, rītimata nadīyāya hailā avatāra

ḷ, lipta śrī⋅gaurāṅga⋅tanu śrī⋅haricandane
ḹ, līlāvalī sabe heri’ haya acetane

e, emana dayālu prabhu nāhi ha’be āra
ai, aikāntika kṛṣṇa-bhakti karila pracāra

o, oḍhradeśe jāiyā prabhu bahu līlā kaila
au, audārya⋅guṇete sārva-bhaume nistārila

caturdaśa svarāvalī je kare kīrtana
acire labhaye sei gaurāṅga⋅caraṇa

śrī⋅jāhnavā⋅rāmacandra⋅pada kari’ āśa
caturdaśa svarāvalī gāya premadāsa
en_translation:
A - Gaurāṅgasundara is a treasure trove of endless virtues. Ā - He is always immersed in joy, an ocean of divine love.

I - The charm of His face surpasses the moon. Ī - The Supreme Lord, whom Brahmā and others meditate upon constantly.

U - He delivered the people of the world by giving the wealth of love. Ū - He did not consider anyone less, whether sinner or distressed.

Ṛ - To repay the debt to Śrīmatī Rādhā⋅rāṇī, Ṝ - He properly incarnated in Nadīyā.

Ḷ - Śrī Gaurāṅga's body is anointed with sandalwood paste. Ḹ - Seeing His divine pastimes, everyone becomes enraptured.

E - There will never be another Lord so merciful. Ai - He preached exclusive devotion to Lord Kṛṣṇa.

O - Going to the land of Odisha, the Lord performed many pastimes. Au - By His quality of magnanimity, He delivered Sārvabhauma Bhaṭṭācārya.

Whoever chants these fourteen vowels soon attains the lotus feet of Lord Gaurāṅga.

With hope in the feet of Śrī Jāhnavā and Rāmacandra, Premadāsa sings these fourteen vowels.
